steps.title: Identify Brown's finding and the mechanism behind it
steps.content:
The passage states that high moonlight inhibits Arizona pocket mouse activity because the benefit (no meaningful increase in foraging success) does not offset the cost (higher chance of being spotted by predatory owls or hawks). The key phrase is "greater lunar intensity may not enable the mice to increase foraging success enough to offset the higher chance of detection."

Now eliminate each choice:
  • Choice A — 'more active during high lunar intensity because it increases foraging without making them easier to detect': the text says the opposite — activity is inhibited, not increased. ✗
  • Choice B — 'reduce activity because predators detect them more easily than they detect three-striped night monkeys': introduces a species comparison the text never makes — monkeys are mentioned for having the opposite response, not for being harder to detect. ✗
  • Choice C — 'reduce activity because predators spot them more easily and the conditions don't benefit the mice enough to justify that risk': matches the two-part mechanism exactly: increased detection risk + insufficient foraging benefit. ✓
  • Choice D — 'monkeys show a different response because risks to monkeys are greater under bright conditions': the text says monkeys rely heavily on visual foraging, giving them a different benefit-risk balance — not that their risks are greater. ✗

steps.title: Confirm and close
steps.content:
Choice C captures both parts of Brown's finding: brighter moonlight raises detection risk for the mice, and that risk is not offset by any meaningful foraging advantage.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
